summary: Large incremental item decomp/matching PR touching 30 files, mostly src/melee/it and item TUs, with supporting symbols/splits/configure changes. The PR body explicitly frames the work as progress toward matching all item TUs and acknowledges temporary gross code. The automated report shows +0.40% matched code, 31 new matches, 59 unmatched-item improvements, one broken match in itmewtwoshadowball .sdata2, and three unmatched-item regressions.

what_changed: Touched 30 files with 1255 additions and 815 deletions, concentrated in item source files and item headers.
configure.py marks melee/it/items/itsscope.c and melee/it/items/it_2E5A.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
config/GALE01/symbols.txt changes several data/sdata/sdata2 symbols from global address-style names to local @ labels and adds a local ...data.0 label.
config/GALE01/splits.txt narrows itmewtwoshadowball .sdata end from 0x804D5580 to 0x804D557D.
Stage files grcorneria.c and grvenom.c remove casts from gobj arguments passed to it_802E7654, suggesting the item helper surface became more typed.

it_266F.c includes matching-oriented rewrites: signature adjustment for __shr2u, extra temporaries, byte-pointer expression, increased PAD_STACK, local ItemSpawnTables overlay, and item-var reinterpret macro.
Automated bot report records broad matching progress: +15688 matched-code bytes, 31 new matches, 59 improvements in unmatched items, one broken match, and three regressions in unmatched items.
smart_moves: Kept the PR scope framed as incremental progress rather than claiming all item TUs were complete; the body records remaining item TUs and functions.
Updated matching configuration only for specific TUs evidenced by new matches: itsscope.c and it_2E5A.c.
Carried symbol and split metadata changes alongside code changes where the bot report indicates data and section matching were affected.
Removed integer casts from stage item-spawn calls in grcorneria.c and grvenom.c, making callsites align better with typed gobj parameters.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Incremental matching PRs can produce real progress while still carrying temporary ugly or tactic-heavy code; record the author’s uncertainty and the bot regressions instead of treating the code shape as a final style precedent.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says code is getting pretty gross and could keep hideous stuff local until 100%
text_corpus: bot report shows progress plus one broken match and three regressions

decomp_lessons.lesson: Changing item helper signatures should be followed through to stage callsites so callers no longer need stale casts.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grcorneria.c removes (s32) gobj casts in it_802E7654 calls
diff_excerpt: grvenom.c removes (s32) gobj casts in it_802E7654 calls

decomp_lessons.lesson: When item TUs approach matching, source edits may need coordinated metadata/config updates, but automated reports should be checked for data-section regressions before generalizing the pattern.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: configure.py flips itsscope.c and it_2E5A.c to Matching
diff_excerpt: symbols.txt and splits.txt metadata changes
text_corpus: bot report shows itmewtwoshadowball .sdata2 broken match and sdata2 regressions in itlinkboomerang

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style or generated local labels such as it_804D5570 and @249 are preserved/adjusted as metadata names rather than replaced with semantic guesses.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt changes multiple it_804D and grHeal globals to local @ labels
decomp_standards: conservative-naming and no-define-alias-global-renames

naming_conventions.convention: Local role names and existing subsystem prefixes remain in use: gobj, gp, pos/sp88, tables, common, monster, it_8026... address-style function names.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grcorneria.c and grvenom.c callsites use gobj/gp/pos/sp88
diff_excerpt: it_266F.c local ItemSpawnTables pointer named tables with common/monster fields

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use narrow PAD_STACK adjustment to recover stack shape in it_8026C75C.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK(4) changed to PAD_STACK(16) in it_8026C75C
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded standards require explicit build/objdiff/regression evidence for PAD_STACK; the PR slice has bot progress but no per-function objdiff rationale.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce temporaries or preserve computed values to influence MWCC expression/lifetime shape.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: it_8026C530 adds last = arg3 - 1
diff_excerpt: it_8026CD50 adds new_var = It_Kind_L_Gun_Ray and moves mask_low assignment
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Evidence supports the edit occurred, not that this is generally preferable style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a local overlay typedef to access a contiguous global allocation as structured tables.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: it_8026CD50 adds local ItemSpawnTables typedef and casts &it_804A0E30
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is a type-recovery bridge candidate; curator should not promote as a general standard without current-source review.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a macro to reinterpret item variable storage when direct union field access does not match desired layout.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: #define it_8026EECC_VARS(ip) (*(it_266F_ItemVars**) &((ip)->xDD4_itemVar.it_266F))
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is potentially in tension with typed-fields-over-pointer-math; PR evidence only shows it was used for matching progress.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Tune symbol scope and local labels for data/sdata/sdata2 matching.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt converts numerous global data and sdata2 symbols to local @ labels
text_corpus: bot report includes matched data improvement but also data regressions
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Data metadata edits should remain evidence-backed and reviewed for section regressions.

notes: The loaded raw diff is truncated, so many changed files are known from metadata but not inspected at hunk-level.
No human review comments are present; review feedback is limited to the automated decomp-dev report and the PR author body.
The bot report supports progress/regression claims, but the slice does not include narrow objdiff/build command output for individual matching tactics.
The author explicitly called some code gross/hideous, so tactic-heavy code should be treated as historical matching evidence, not as an accepted style precedent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Treat tactic-heavy incremental item matching PRs as progress evidence, not final style precedent
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2516 produced substantial item matching progress while the author explicitly described the code as gross/hideous and the bot reported both gains and regressions. Curators should preserve the distinction between historical matching tactics and reusable accepted style.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author describes gross/hideous code while closing in on matching all item TUs
text_corpus: bot report shows 31 new matches, 59 improvements, one broken match, and three regressions

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed helper signature updates should be propagated to cross-system callers
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR removed stale integer casts at grcorneria/grvenom callsites for it_802E7654, showing that item helper signature cleanup can require stage caller updates.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grcorneria.c removes (s32) gobj casts
diff_excerpt: grvenom.c removes (s32) gobj casts

source_update_candidates.title: No new standard requested: PAD_STACK and item-var reinterpret macros need existing tactic caution
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2516 uses PAD_STACK(16), a local overlay typedef, and an item-var reinterpret macro during item matching. This appears covered by existing standards on matching tactics and typed fields; no new standard should be promoted from this PR alone.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK(4) to PAD_STACK(16)
diff_excerpt: local ItemSpawnTables typedef
diff_excerpt: it_8026EECC_VARS reinterpret macro
decomp_standards: matching-tactics-need-evidence
decomp_standards: typed-fields-over-pointer-math
source_update_candidates.reason: Route as a source-review note rather than an accepted new standard because PR evidence lacks narrow objdiff rationale.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that PAD_STACK(16), pointer-byte casts, or item-var reinterpret macros are accepted global style; PR body says the code is gross and the loaded standards require explicit evidence for such tactics.
Do not claim all item TUs were matched; the PR body states 21 item TUs and 112 item functions remained.
Do not claim no regressions; the bot report lists one broken match and three regressions in unmatched items.
Do not infer reviewer acceptance rationale or human review intent; no review comments or reviews are loaded.
